opportunities.title: Another Day, Another Oracle: Bonzo Lend's $9M Hedera Exploit Was Foretold in Every Postmortem You Ignored
opportunities.why_now: A fresh $9M oracle exploit on Hedera just gave the channel a live case study in the same failure mode that's drained lending protocols for years.
opportunities.angle: Incident teardown: walk through the oracle manipulation mechanics, then map it against 3-4 prior oracle exploits to show the pattern nobody fixed

opportunities.title: No, AI Didn't Cause a DeFi 'Hackpocalypse' — Humans Still Ship the Same Bugs
opportunities.why_now: A Dragonfly partner just publicly pushed back on AI-hacking fear narratives right as a real oracle exploit (Bonzo) proves the boring old vulnerabilities are still the ones draining funds.
opportunities.angle: Contrarian security take: pair the Dragonfly comment with the Bonzo exploit as evidence — the threat isn't AI, it's the same unaudited oracle dependencies

opportunities.title: $288M in Seized Bitcoin Just Landed on Coinbase Prime — Here's What That Custody Move Actually Signals
opportunities.why_now: Arkham flagged a massive government wallet transfer to Coinbase Prime today, and on-chain forensics-watchers want to know what happens next to seized funds.
opportunities.angle: On-chain tracing explainer: what custody-to-exchange movement typically precedes (liquidation, auction, cold storage), with wallet-level evidence

opportunities.title: Chainalysis Just Cleared the Daubert Bar — Why That Makes Every On-Chain Forensics Claim in Your Next Court Case Fair Game
opportunities.why_now: Chainalysis's fresh blog on meeting the Daubert standard in the Sterlingov case is a legal precedent that changes how blockchain evidence gets weaponized in prosecutions and civil suits.
opportunities.angle: Explainer for security-literate readers: what Daubert means, why it matters for privacy-coin users and mixers, and what evidentiary standard just got easier to clear

opportunities.title: A Dirty Cop Went to Jail Protecting a Crypto 'Mogul' — The Adam Iza Case Is a Masterclass in How Extortion Rackets Actually Get Built
opportunities.why_now: A former LA sheriff's deputy was just jailed for obstructing the Adam Iza extortion probe, exposing law-enforcement complicity in crypto-adjacent crime.
opportunities.angle: True-crime-style breakdown of the extortion scheme and the corrupt protection network, framed as a warning about who profits when crypto crime meets local law enforcement
